title: Intensely Fermented Russian Mincemeat Dumplings
description: A traditional Russian dish featuring fermented mincemeat, this recipe is a keto-friendly hangover cure, with a silky texture and intense flavor. It is typically served as a snack or light meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the mincemeat, sour cream, eggs, onion, garlic, salt, black pepper, and dried dill, mixing well.
Knead the mixture for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let it ferment in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours.
When ready to cook, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the fermented mixture into the boiling water.
Cook the dumplings for 10-12 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and serve hot.
Traditionally, the dumplings are served with a side of sour cream or melted butter.
Let the dumplings cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
